title_en: Donald Trump’s transformation of the Department of Justice will be hard to undo
title_zh: 唐纳德·特朗普对司法部的改造将很难逆转
rubric_en: Political prosecutions are just part of a more radical reinvention
rubric_zh: 政治起诉只是更激进重塑的一部分

paragraphs.en: Anyone who has ever worked as a lawyer for Donald Trump makes a bet. There is the possibility that you, too, will run afoul of the law—eight of the president’s lawyers have themselves been indicted. Alternatively, your work might pay dividends. Such was Todd Blanche’s calculus three years ago when his white-shoe firm gave him a choice: represent Mr Trump, then a candidate and a criminal defendant, or remain a partner. Mr Blanche chose Mr Trump. Now the president has selected him to be America’s top cop.
paragraphs.zh: 任何曾为唐纳德·特朗普担任律师的人，都是在下注。一种可能是，你自己也会触犯法律——总统的律师中已有八人被起诉。另一种可能是，你的工作会带来回报。三年前，陶德·布兰奇的白鞋律所给了他一个选择：要么代理当时既是候选人又是刑事被告的特朗普先生，要么继续留任合伙人。这就是布兰奇先生当时的盘算。布兰奇先生选择了特朗普先生。如今，总统已选中他出任美国最高执法官。

paragraphs.en: On July 15th the Senate began hearings to consider Mr Blanche’s nomination to lead the Department of Justice (DoJ). Whether lawmakers confirm him as attorney-general will test their willingness to endorse Mr Trump’s legal agenda, but even a rebuke would be only symbolic. The president will still get his way at the DoJ; Mr Blanche can stay in the job on an acting basis for months and be replaced by someone similar after.
paragraphs.zh: 7月15日，参议院开始举行听证会，审议布兰奇先生出任司法部（DoJ）部长的提名。议员们是否确认他担任司法部长，将考验他们是否愿意为特朗普先生的法律议程背书，但即便否决，也只具有象征意义。总统仍会在司法部得偿所愿；布兰奇先生可以以代理身份留任数月，之后再由类似的人接替。

paragraphs.en: With or without Mr Blanche, the president will continue to lead a transformation of dramatic scale. With warrants and subpoenas, the DoJ is pursuing Mr Trump’s critics and his hobby-horses, from political adversaries to election fraud to leaks in the press. The number of lawyers has dwindled by 20%; former prosecutors point to an incompatibility between the president’s agenda and the fair application of law. The DoJ is retreating from certain types of criminal enforcement. And, increasingly, judges are telling government lawyers that they cannot be taken at their word.
paragraphs.zh: 无论有没有布兰奇先生，总统都将继续主导一场规模巨大的转型。司法部正通过搜查令和传票追查特朗普先生的批评者及其执念所在，从政治对手到选举舞弊，再到媒体泄密。律师人数已减少20%；前检察官指出，总统的议程与法律的公正适用互不相容。司法部正在退出某些类型的刑事执法。而且，法官们越来越多地告诉政府律师，不能只凭他们的话就采信。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ’s duties, at least as historically defined, include enforcing the law and defending the government in court. Ever since Richard Nixon urged his attorney-general to interfere with an investigation, politicians of both parties have generally agreed that the DoJ should operate at some distance from the presidency. Mr Trump has bucked that consensus, claiming that Democrats weaponised the law against him. Joe Biden’s DoJ indicted him twice, elected Democratic district-attorneys indicted him twice more and Letitia James, New York’s attorney-general, sued him in civil court.
paragraphs.zh: 至少按照历史定义，司法部的职责包括执法以及在法庭上为政府辩护。自理查德·尼克松敦促其司法部长干预一项调查以来，两党政客大体上都认同，司法部应与总统保持一定距离。特朗普先生打破了这一共识，声称民主党人把法律武器化，用来对付他。乔·拜登领导下的司法部两次起诉他，民选的民主党地方检察官又两次起诉他，纽约州总检察长利蒂希娅·詹姆斯则在民事法庭起诉他。

paragraphs.en: Pam Bondi, Mr Trump’s first attorney-general, was fired in April for being insufficiently aggressive. Mr Blanche told the Senate this week that he is not the president’s “yes man”. But shortly after assuming his current post, Mr Blanche was touting a second indictment against James Comey, a former FBI director and antagonist of the president. Mr Comey had posted a photo of seashells on a beach arranged like “86 47”. To “86” someone means to get rid of them; Mr Trump is the 47th president. The DoJ contends that Mr Comey was threatening to kill Mr Trump.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生的首任司法部长帕姆·邦迪因不够强硬，于4月被解职。布兰奇先生本周告诉参议院，他不是总统的“唯命是从者”。但在就任现职后不久，布兰奇先生就在宣扬对詹姆斯·科米的第二项起诉。科米是联邦调查局前局长，也是总统的对头。科米曾发布一张照片，照片中海滩上的贝壳被摆成“86 47”的样子。在英语里，“86”某人意为除掉此人；特朗普先生是第47任总统。司法部主张，科米是在威胁杀害特朗普先生。

paragraphs.en: This prosecution is almost certain to fail, just like the president’s cases against Ms James; Jerome Powell, the former chair of the Federal Reserve; and six Democratic lawmakers. Last month a court tossed subpoenas targeting Tim Walz, Minnesota’s governor, and other Democratic officials in that state. The judge wrote that the subpoenas were “not issued to investigate, but to harass, coerce and retaliate” for the officials’ refusal to aid the president’s immigration crackdown.
paragraphs.zh: 这项起诉几乎肯定会失败，就像总统针对詹姆斯女士、前美联储主席杰罗姆·鲍威尔以及六名民主党议员的案子一样。上个月，一家法院撤销了针对明尼苏达州州长蒂姆·沃尔兹以及该州其他民主党官员的传票。法官写道，这些传票“不是为了调查而发出，而是为了骚扰、胁迫和报复”，因为这些官员拒绝协助总统的移民严打行动。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump complements prosecutions of political foes with lenient treatment of his friends, through pardons and dropped charges. Last year political appointees at the DoJ ordered prosecutors to abandon a corruption case against Eric Adams, then New York’s mayor. Unlike Mr Walz, he had agreed to co-operate on immigration. A judge in New York wants to know whether a quid pro quo inspired the DoJ to drop fraud charges against Gautam Adani, an Indian billionaire who has promised to invest $10bn in America.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生一边起诉政敌，一边通过赦免和撤销指控宽待朋友。去年，司法部的政治任命官员命令检察官放弃对时任纽约市长埃里克·亚当斯的腐败案。与沃尔兹先生不同，亚当斯同意在移民问题上合作。纽约一名法官想知道，司法部撤销针对印度亿万富翁高塔姆·阿达尼的欺诈指控，是否出于某种交换条件；阿达尼曾承诺在美国投资100亿美元。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump had sought to reward allies most explicitly through a $1.8bn fund for supposed victims of government lawfare. Even Republican senators balked at that; in June Mr Blanche said he had given up the idea. But it loomed over his confirmation hearing, with John Cornyn, a Republican senator, observing that his answers “don’t lead inevitably to the conclusion that it’s dead”.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生曾试图通过一个18亿美元的基金，最明目张胆地奖赏盟友；该基金据称用于补偿政府“法律战”的受害者。即便共和党参议员也对此退缩了；6月，布兰奇先生说他已经放弃这一想法。但这件事仍笼罩着他的确认听证会，共和党参议员约翰·科宁指出，他的回答“并不必然导向它已经死了这一结论”。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ is vast; last year it charged 81,000 people. Politicised probes mark a radical departure from past norms, but they are not enough to upend that work. Yet broader change is underway, too, with the department itself depleted and redirected, at remarkable speed.
paragraphs.zh: 司法部规模庞大；去年它起诉了8.1万人。政治化调查标志着对过往规范的激进背离，但还不足以颠覆这些工作。然而，更广泛的变化也在进行：该部门本身正以惊人的速度被削弱和改道。

paragraphs.en: Mass attrition means that some of the most experienced lawyers have left, often for better pay in the private sector. A former prosecutor in the Virginia office that first indicted Mr Comey recounts how, after charges were issued, nearly everyone in his unit started looking for new work. “These people have job options.” Now the DoJ is struggling to fill its ranks, lowering hiring standards to take applicants straight from law school and offering some recruits $25,000 signing bonuses.
paragraphs.zh: 大规模人员流失意味着一些最有经验的律师已经离开，往往是去了薪酬更高的私营部门。曾在最早起诉科米先生的弗吉尼亚办公室任职的一名前检察官回忆说，指控提出后，他所在部门几乎所有人都开始找新工作。“这些人有工作选择。”如今，司法部正苦于填补人员空缺，不得不降低招聘标准，直接从法学院招收申请者，并向一些新员工提供2.5万美元的签约奖金。

paragraphs.en: Units specialising in cryptocurrency fraud and corruption by public officials have been gutted. (As it happens, Mr Trump made more than $1.4bn from cryptocurrency last year.) The group focused on counterintelligence and enforcing export controls has warned Congress of “unprecedented personnel constraints”, with a 40% drop in prosecutors from a year and a half ago. Up to a third of the counterterrorism section has left, says a former prosecutor in that unit. The FBI, which sits within the DoJ, has lost about 300 special agents who worked on national security.
paragraphs.zh: 专门处理加密货币欺诈和公职人员腐败的部门已被大幅削弱。（巧的是，特朗普先生去年从加密货币中赚了逾14亿美元。）负责反情报和执行出口管制的小组已向国会警告称，他们面临“前所未有的人员限制”，检察官人数较一年半前下降40%。一名曾在反恐部门任职的前检察官说，该部门多达三分之一的人员已经离开。隶属于司法部的联邦调查局也流失了约300名从事国家安全工作的特别探员。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ’s national-security division has expertise that most prosecutors lack, in handling classified information and charging complex statutes. They sift through dozens of FBI referrals and decide which to pursue. “What feels real versus which ones are idle chatter? When is the right time to disrupt a plot? Do we go now? Prosecutors learn that only by working these cases over many years,” says one who left last year.
paragraphs.zh: 司法部国家安全司拥有多数检察官所不具备的专业能力，能够处理机密信息并依据复杂法规提出指控。他们会筛查数十份联邦调查局移送材料，并决定追查哪些案件。“哪些感觉是真的，哪些只是空谈？什么时候才是破坏一起阴谋的正确时机？我们现在就行动吗？检察官只有多年办理这些案件，才能学会这一点，”一位去年离职的人说。

paragraphs.en: Fewer resources mean less enforcement in some domains. White-collar defence lawyers remark how work has slowed. Last year the number of financial-fraud indictments out of DoJ headquarters and the US attorney’s office in Manhattan fell by 30% from the ten-year average. Indictments are a lagging indicator of enforcement activity. Subpoenas of financial firms, which precede them, are “not happening, basically,” says a white-collar lawyer in New York, who expects even fewer indictments to come. “Nobody’s investigating those things.” Cases targeting political graft have largely dried up. Mr Trump is notably lax about that, having granted clemency to at least 20 politicians convicted of self-dealing over his two terms.
paragraphs.zh: 资源减少意味着某些领域的执法力度下降。白领犯罪辩护律师注意到，相关工作已经放缓。去年，司法部总部和曼哈顿联邦检察官办公室提出的金融欺诈起诉数量，较十年平均水平下降30%。起诉数量是执法活动的滞后指标。纽约一名白领犯罪律师说，作为起诉前置步骤的对金融公司的传票“基本上没有发生”，他预计未来起诉还会更少。“没人再调查那些事了。”针对政治腐败的案件基本枯竭。特朗普先生在这方面尤其宽松；在他的两个任期内，他已对至少20名因以权谋私被定罪的政客给予宽宥。

paragraphs.en: Instead the DoJ has made a big show around the president’s bugbears: health-care and benefits fraud; anything connected to cartels; transgender care; diversity, equity and inclusion; and election fraud. Recently the FBI dispatched 260 analysts to investigate debunked claims of vote-rigging in the 2020 election in Georgia, which Mr Trump maintains he won. In January the FBI seized records related to that race in Georgia’s Fulton County. Last week a judge quashed the DoJ’s subpoena seeking names of poll workers there: an “overly broad fishing expedition is bad and is not allowed”, he said.
paragraphs.zh: 相反，司法部围绕总统的心头大患大张旗鼓：医疗保健和福利欺诈；任何与贩毒集团有关的事项；跨性别医疗；多元、公平和包容；以及选举舞弊。最近，联邦调查局派出260名分析员，调查有关2020年佐治亚州选举舞弊的已被驳倒说法；特朗普先生坚称自己赢得了该州。1月，联邦调查局查封了佐治亚州富尔顿县与那场选举有关的记录。上周，一名法官撤销了司法部要求提供当地投票工作人员姓名的传票；他说，一场“过于宽泛的摸鱼式搜查是糟糕的，也是不被允许的”。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ will be at the tip of the spear if Mr Trump attempts to intervene in the midterm elections in November. Ominously, it has all but shut the unit that normally monitors election-related crimes. A prosecutor who left that section last year says he thinks the administration is “taking steps to be in a position to put its thumb on the scale in 2026 or 2028”.
paragraphs.zh: 如果特朗普先生试图干预11月的中期选举，司法部将站在最前线。令人不安的是，它几乎关闭了通常负责监督选举相关犯罪的部门。一名去年离开该部门的检察官说，他认为本届政府正在“采取步骤，让自己处于能够在2026年或2028年偏袒一方的位置”。

paragraphs.en: Election-year training for FBI agents and DoJ staff, once mandatory, has been cancelled. The department seems to have no intention to stand up the National Election Command Post, which normally monitors irregularities. Instead, election deniers populate the DoJ, including several who worked to overturn the 2020 result. Recently they threatened to prosecute election officials who let non-citizens vote.
paragraphs.zh: 曾经强制要求的联邦调查局探员和司法部工作人员选举年培训已被取消。司法部似乎无意设立通常用于监测异常情况的全国选举指挥站。相反，司法部内充斥着否认选举结果的人，其中包括数名曾参与推翻2020年结果的人。最近，他们威胁要起诉允许非公民投票的选举官员。

paragraphs.en: No issue has consumed the attention of the Trump administration like immigration. By September the FBI had diverted a fifth of its roughly 14,000 agents to immigration enforcement. What is striking is not that the president has made immigration a priority—he said he would do as much—but the manner in which DoJ lawyers are pursuing those cases.
paragraphs.zh: 没有哪个议题像移民一样占据特朗普政府的注意力。到9月，联邦调查局已将其约1.4万名探员中的五分之一转用于移民执法。引人注目的并不是总统把移民列为优先事项——他早就说过会这么做——而是司法部律师推进这些案件的方式。

paragraphs.en: In the autumn Mr Blanche showed up in Chicago, the site of a surge of agents dubbed “Operation Midway Blitz”, and decried “an organised effort by domestic terrorists to actually injure and hurt” those agents. Then his deputy told prosecutors to “go big and go loud” against protesters.
paragraphs.zh: 秋天，布兰奇先生现身芝加哥，那里正经历一波被称为“中途闪击行动”的探员增派。他谴责称，有“国内恐怖分子有组织地实际伤害并袭击”这些探员。随后，他的副手告诉检察官，要对抗议者“声势浩大地大干一场”。

paragraphs.en: A prosecutor in Chicago who left earlier this year says that every unit there, including ones focused on cyber and national-security crime, was dragooned into protester cases, which became a “dreaded thing”. He says the pressure from the front office to file charges was so great that prosecutors had to present a compelling argument not to do so. That is the inverse of how decisions are usually made.
paragraphs.zh: 一名今年早些时候离开芝加哥的检察官说，当地每个部门，包括专注网络犯罪和国家安全犯罪的部门，都被强行拉去处理抗议者案件，而这些案件成了“令人畏惧的事情”。他说，前台办公室施加的起诉压力大到检察官必须提出有说服力的理由，才能不提起指控。这与通常的决策方式正好相反。

paragraphs.en: Agents arrested nearly 4,000 immigrants during Midway Blitz. The crackdown in Chicago also became a stark example of how to drive away lawyers. In the US attorney’s office, which has seven criminal sections, each chief serving at the start of Midway Blitz has quit. Seven of their 15 deputies and at least a quarter of the 90 or so staff prosecutors have left, too.
paragraphs.zh: 在中途闪击行动期间，探员逮捕了近4000名移民。芝加哥的严打也成为赶走律师的鲜明例子。在拥有七个刑事部门的联邦检察官办公室中，中途闪击行动开始时任职的每一位部门主管都已辞职。他们15名副手中的7人，以及约90名一线检察官中至少四分之一的人，也已经离开。

paragraphs.en: Across the country, there have been hundreds of shaky cases brought against protesters. Many collapse before trial. A jury rebellion awaits those that do make it that far. Ten of 13 resulted in an acquittal as of March, according to Steven Salky, a defence lawyer who tracks unusual charging decisions. By contrast, across all federal trials last year, the acquittal rate was 12%.
paragraphs.zh: 在全国各地，针对抗议者提起的站不住脚的案件已有数百起。许多在审判前就崩溃了。那些真正走到审判阶段的案件，则面临陪审团的反抗。据追踪异常起诉决定的辩护律师史蒂文·索尔基统计，截至3月，13起案件中有10起以无罪判决告终。相比之下，去年所有联邦审判的无罪判决率为12%。

paragraphs.en: Some cases have veered into the absurd. A jury took 35 minutes to acquit a man accused of pointing a laser at the president’s helicopter. Once unusual practices are more common. In October prosecutors charged six Democratic activists and politicians in Chicago with conspiring to impede a federal agent, in a case known as the “Broadview Six”. After a judge reviewed transcripts from the grand-jury sitting—held in secret, without judges or defence lawyers—she said she had never seen such misbehaviour by prosecutors. Among other no-nos, they had dismissed sceptical jurors (including one who called the case “a crock of shit”) who might have been unlikely to return an indictment. In May prosecutors dropped the case.
paragraphs.zh: 有些案件已经滑向荒唐。一个陪审团只用了35分钟，就宣告一名被控用激光照射总统直升机的男子无罪。曾经少见的做法如今更常见了。10月，检察官指控六名芝加哥民主党活动人士和政客密谋阻碍一名联邦探员，该案被称为“布罗德维尤六人案”。一名法官审阅了大陪审团听证的记录后表示，她从未见过检察官有如此不当行为；大陪审团听证秘密进行，没有法官或辩护律师在场。除其他禁忌行为外，他们还排除了持怀疑态度的陪审员，其中包括一名称该案是“一派胡言”的陪审员；这些陪审员本来可能不太可能同意起诉。5月，检察官撤销了该案。

paragraphs.en: A consequence of all this is that the DoJ is losing credibility in the courts. Increasingly judges are calling out lapses by government lawyers, saying they cannot take them at face value. The judge overseeing the Broadview Six case said she believed that “most government attorneys are doing the best they can to do the right thing”. Then she added: “That trust has been broken.” More judges are now requiring depositions and documents to verify that the government’s claims are true, and threatening sanctions when its lawyers obfuscate or fail to comply. In the first 14 months of Mr Trump’s second term, according to Just Security, a site for legal commentary, the DoJ gave courts inaccurate information in nearly 100 instances.
paragraphs.zh: 这一切的一个后果是，司法部正在法庭上失去公信力。法官们越来越多地点名指出政府律师的失误，称不能照单全收他们的说法。负责布罗德维尤六人案的法官说，她相信“大多数政府律师都在尽最大努力做正确的事”。接着她补充道：“这种信任已经破裂。”现在，更多法官要求通过证词录取和文件来核实政府主张是否属实，并在其律师含糊其词或不遵守要求时威胁施加制裁。法律评论网站Just Security称，在特朗普先生第二任期的前14个月里，司法部向法院提供不准确信息近100次。

paragraphs.en: The problem is acute in immigration cases. The administration’s mass-detention policy led to a twentyfold spike in “habeas” petitions by detained immigrants suing for release between 2024 and 2025. Just Security found nearly 800 instances of non-compliance with court orders in habeas cases, and 13 sanctions and contempt-of-court findings against DoJ lawyers. The administration’s response has been to label any judge who disagrees with it a “rogue activist”. Mr Blanche has called it a “war” on the judiciary.
paragraphs.zh: 这个问题在移民案件中尤为严重。本届政府的大规模拘押政策，导致被拘押移民为寻求获释而提起的“人身保护令”申请在2024年至2025年间激增20倍。Just Security发现，在人身保护令案件中，有近800起不遵守法院命令的情形，并有13项针对司法部律师的制裁和藐视法庭认定。政府的回应是把任何不同意它的法官都称为“流氓活动分子”。布兰奇先生称这是对司法机构的“战争”。

paragraphs.en: Seen one way, that fighting talk reflects something positive: the guardrails in the judicial system holding up, to the administration’s dismay. Already the department’s alumni are asking what it will take to reconstitute it when Mr Trump leaves office. A former prosecutor says he and his former colleagues want to return. Still, the appeal of the department diminishes somewhat with the prospect that the next person overseeing it may sack you, or harness the law for their personal ends. For decades the DoJ enjoyed some protection from the politics that have fractured America. That era seems over. ■
paragraphs.zh: 从一个角度看，这种好斗言辞反映了一件积极的事：司法体系中的护栏仍在发挥作用，令政府懊恼。司法部的前成员们已经在询问，特朗普先生离任后，重建该部门需要什么。一名前检察官说，他和昔日同事都想回去。不过，如果想到下一位主管司法部的人可能会解雇你，或把法律用于个人目的，这个部门的吸引力仍会有所下降。几十年来，司法部在一定程度上免受撕裂美国的政治影响。那个时代似乎结束了。■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of American politics with The US in brief, our daily newsletter with fast analysis of the most important political news, and Checks and Balance, a weekly note that examines the state of American democracy and the issues that matter to voters.
paragraphs.zh: 通过我们的每日简报《美国简讯》及时掌握美国政治动态，它会快速分析最重要的政治新闻；还可阅读每周一期的《制衡》，了解美国民主状况以及选民关心的问题。
